assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30001.md: '## Classification: CODE CHANGE REQUEST' and '## Action

Sub-task creation required to address the transaction wrapping fix.' Report table row: '| 30001 | ... | CODE CHANGE REQUEST | Sub-task created for transaction wrapping |'. subtask-1.md exists and references comment 30001.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '## Classification: SUGGESTION' and reasoning states 'The reviewer uses suggestive language throughout: "should also add" and "would help". The phrase "should also" proposes an addition beyond what the task required, and "would help" frames the index as a performance enhancement rather than a mandatory fix.'

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: '## Classification: NIT' and 'No sub-task is created for nits.' Report table: '| 30003 | ... | NIT | No sub-task -- minor wording improvement for error context message |'. No subtask file exists for comment 30003.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: '## Classification: QUESTION' and 'No sub-task is created for questions.' Report table: '| 30004 | ... | QUESTION | No sub-task -- reviewer asks for clarification on GET behavior for deleted SBOMs |'. No subtask file exists for comment 30004.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1, 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7, wrapping UPDATE statements in transaction), '## Files to Modify' (line 13), '## Implementation Notes' (line 16), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 24).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 8) with URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 9).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 34) with '**Comment ID:** 30001', '**Author:** reviewer-a', '**File:** modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs line 60', and the full original comment text quoted: '> The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-1.md), and a report (report.md). No source code files appear in the outputs. The report describes reading and verifying code but does not mention modifying any code files.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report's Review Comment Classifications table lists all 4 comments with their classifications and actions. The report header states '4 review comments classified: 1 code change request (sub-task created), 1 suggestion (not upgraded), 1 nit, 1 question'.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's overall status is 'WARN' with issues requiring attention. There is no mention of merging, auto-merging, or any merge action in the report. The report only describes verification checks, classifications, and sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: Report table row: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file adding 5 integration tests; no existing test files were modified or deleted |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility' section that evaluates: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check - found no documented convention for indexes on soft-delete columns, (2) Codebase pattern check - insufficient evidence of consistent index creation pattern in migrations, (3) Performance-related scrutiny - explicitly states general industry best practices alone are insufficient for upgrade. Report table also notes: 'No sub-task -- not upgraded (no matching convention in CONVENTIONS.md or counted codebase pattern)'.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md concludes: 'The suggestion remains classified as SUGGESTION because: No matching convention exists in CONVENTIONS.md, No counted codebase pattern demonstrates consistent index creation in migrations... No sub-task is created for suggestions that are not upgraded to code change requests.' No subtask file exists for comment 30002. Report table: '| 30002 | ... | SUGGESTION | No sub-task -- not upgraded (no matching convention in CONVENTIONS.md or counted codebase pattern) |'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: Report Test Quality row states: 'Eval Quality: N/A'. The full row is: '| Test Quality | PASS | All 5 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive test patterns detected (each test has distinct setup/assertion logic); Eval Quality: N/A |'. Eval Quality is marked N/A and does not affect the overall Test Quality PASS result.
